lesson.menu_title: Block 3 -- Leading Change & the Change Team
lesson.menu_desc: Lead people through the change: what the team must, has to, should, and needs to do -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Block 3 -- Leading Change & the Change Team
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Vinicius Vale Bassan | Aula 12 -- Leading Change & the Change Team

listenings.text: Carlos, the integration is moving, but now you have to lead the people, not just the plan. Let me brief you on the change side. First, every manager must own the message; nobody can stay silent, because silence creates fear. Second, you have to be visible. People need to see the leaders, so you should walk the floor and answer hard questions in the open. Third, set clear expectations: the teams must hit the milestones, but they need to know it is safe to flag a problem early. We should reward the people who raise risks, not punish them. Fourth, resistance is normal. You do not have to win every argument; you have to listen, then explain the why one more time. Finally, protect the momentum. You must celebrate the small wins out loud, because a change that feels slow loses energy fast. Lead the change the same way you led the deal, but remember: this time the work is people, not numbers.

Hi Carlos, this is Diana from the board, with a quick recap after the town hall today. Overall, very strong. You were visible, you answered the hard questions directly, and the teams felt heard, which is exactly what a change like this needs. A few notes. First, the message: it is clear, but you have to repeat it more often; people only believe a change when they hear it many times. Second, the managers: most of them own the message now, but two are still silent, and you must coach them this week, because a quiet manager creates a nervous team. Third, the risk culture: keep rewarding the people who flag problems early; that is working well, so do not change it. One thing to fix: a few people think the milestones are optional, so make it clear that the dates are firm and the teams have to hit them. Send me your engagement plan by Friday, and we should review the momentum at the end of the month. Strong start on the people side, Carlos. Talk soon.
